channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Convenes Emergency Energy Security Meeting as Hormuz Disruption Threatens Oil Supply
item.description: Premier Li Qiang led a State Council session on energy security as China confronts the impact of the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Xi Jinping called for the strait to remain open in his first public statement on the blocked waterway.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇰🇵 Russia-North Korea Open First Land Bridge Between Countries
item.description: Russia and North Korea held a ceremony for a new 5-kilometer bridge linking the countries by land for the first time. The bridge for cars and trucks marks a deepening of ties between Moscow and Pyongyang.

item.title: 🇪🇪🇺🇦 Estonia Rejects Ukraine Warnings of Russian Threat to Baltics
item.description: Estonian parliament Foreign Affairs Committee chairman Marko Mihkelson pushed back against Ukrainian warnings that the Baltics could be Russias next target, saying such narratives do not help Ukraine.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇱 Hezbollah Fires Rockets at Israeli Troops in Southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military said Hezbollah fired rockets toward its troops in a blatant violation of the ceasefire agreement, as the US-Iran truce deadline nears. Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire multiple times.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Destroys Mass Graves Near Mariupol, Satellite Images Suggest
item.description: Satellite imagery indicates Russia has destroyed mass graves near Mariupol used to bury residents who died during the 2022 siege. Ukraines Center for the Study of Occupation called it evidence of attempted cover-up.

item.title: 🇵🇭 US and Allies Launch Major Combat Drills in Philippines as Japan Joins for First Time
item.description: Over 17,000 US and Filipino troops began the annual Balikatan exercises, with Japan's Self-Defense Forces participating fully for the first time. The drills include live-fire exercises near the South China Sea and Taiwan Strait, signaling strengthened deterrence against Chinese aggression despite US focus on the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Ends Decades-Long Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Major Policy Shift
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's Cabinet approved scrapping Japan's postwar ban on lethal weapons exports, allowing sales of warships and missiles to 17 partner countries. The historic shift aims to bolster Japan's defense industry amid regional security concerns, drawing criticism from China.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump's Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh Faces Senate Grilling on Independence and Wealth
item.description: Kevin Warsh testified before the Senate Banking Committee, pledging to run an independent Federal Reserve while facing intense Democratic scrutiny over his $131-209 million in assets and ties to hedge fund billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller. Elizabeth Warren called him 'uniquely ill-suited' for the role.

item.title: 🥶 US Army Tests New Arctic Vehicles and Drones in Alaska to Counter Russia and China
item.description: CNN gained exclusive access to the 11th Airborne 'Arctic Angels' in northern Alaska as they test next-generation cold-weather vehicles and combat drones. The preparations reflect growing US concern over Russian and Chinese activity in the increasingly strategic Arctic region.

item.title: 🇳🇱✈️🇷🇺 NATO Fighter Jets Intercept Russian Bombers Over Baltic Sea
item.description: French Rafale fighters deployed from Lithuania intercepted Russian bombers and fighter jets flying over the Baltic Sea, marking another escalation in NATO-Russia tensions.

item.title: 🇳🇱🇪🇺⚠️ Dutch Intelligence Warns Europe Must Focus on Own Security
item.description: The Dutch MIVD intelligence agency warned that Europe must take greater responsibility for its own security, citing pressure on Western alliances and China's support for Russia's war in Ukraine.

item.title: 🛢️🚢⛽ Marine Fuel Industry Scrambles for Oil Amid Iran War Disruptions
item.description: Marine fuel providers face tightening supply of heavy sweet crude as the Iran war disrupts Middle East imports, with refiners competing for the same limited supplies.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU expands Iran sanctions to include Hormuz blockade officials
item.description: EU countries agreed to widen sanctions against Iran to target those responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz, according to EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas. The move escalates European pressure on Tehran amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇰🇷 US confirms no missile defense system moved from South Korea amid Iran war
item.description: A US general denied reports that a key missile defense system was relocated from South Korea to the Middle East for the Iran conflict, amid regional concerns about security gaps in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia intensifies coastal mining in Crimea to prevent Ukrainian landings
item.description: Russian forces are mining beaches in occupied Crimea to prevent Ukrainian amphibious operations, according to Ukraine Southern Defense Forces spokesperson. "The beaches will now become minefields," said Vladyslav Voloshyn.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Hezbollah violates ceasefire with rocket attacks on Israeli troops in Lebanon
item.description: The IDF accused Hezbollah of breaching the ceasefire by launching rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon and sending a drone toward Israel, raising concerns about escalation despite the truce.

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Maersk warns ships to avoid Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire
item.description: Danish shipping giant Maersk advised vessels to continue avoiding the Strait of Hormuz despite a temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran, citing ongoing volatility and lack of clarity on safe passage.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Yet to Confirm Attendance at Last-Ditch Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Looms
item.description: Host nation Pakistan says there is still no confirmation that Iran will attend negotiations with the United States, with just one day left on the ceasefire. U.S. forces have boarded an Iranian oil tanker at sea.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Claims New Iran Deal Will Be Better Than 2015 Nuclear Agreement
item.description: President Donald Trump said he believes the nuclear deal currently being negotiated with Iran will be better than the 2015 international agreement that curbed Tehran's nuclear program.

item.title: 💰 Pentagon Seeks Billions for Drones and Air Defenses in 2027 Budget After Iran War
item.description: U.S. military officials are calling for tens of billions of dollars in the next budget year for drones and air defense systems used extensively during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese Student Charged With Illegally Photographing U.S. Military Aircraft
item.description: A college student from China has been charged with illegally taking photos of U.S. military planes in Nebraska during a multistate road trip, raising espionage concerns.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria charges six with treason over alleged coup plot to overthrow Tinubu
item.description: Nigeria filed 13 criminal charges against six former security officials accused of plotting the violent overthrow of President Bola Tinubu, marking a serious political crisis in Africa's most populous nation.

item.title: ☢️ NATO criticises Russian and Chinese nuclear stances, urges cooperation with US
item.description: NATO called out Russian and Chinese nuclear arms policies and urged both countries to work with the United States on stability and transparency at an upcoming international conference amid rising nuclear tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump's China policy drifts as tariffs stall amid Iran war focus
item.description: President Trump's vow to use tariffs to reset relations with China has stalled as the administration focuses on the Iran conflict, leaving US-China policy in limbo.

item.title: 🇨🇦❄️ Canadian military pushes Arctic sovereignty as Trump threatens annexation
item.description: Trump's threats to make Canada the 51st US state have pushed Canada to rethink defense relations, with a focus on demonstrating Arctic sovereignty through independent military operations.
